title: MaxRecord
description: MAXGEN v1.4 — Maxwell Genealogy Standard. A single original source document exactly as found, with no interpretation added. Every MaxPerson assertion traces back to at least one MaxRecord. Schema content stable since v1.1; version stamp follows lockstep MAXGEN release versioning.

record_id.description: Globally unique identifier for this record.

schema_version.description: MAXGEN version this record conforms to.

redistribution_license.description: Whether this record can be redistributed. tier2-private means never include in open dataset.

is_living_flag.description: True if any person in this record may be living. Triggers 404 on all open endpoints.

persons_mentioned.description: All persons referenced in this document, as written in the source.

name_as_written.description: Name exactly as it appears in the source document.

role.description: Role this person plays in the document.

alternate_names.description: Variant spellings or alternate names for persons mentioned, as they appear across multiple documents.

record_date.description: Date range of the event recorded. Never store a single year unless certain.

transcription.description: Full text transcription of the document content.

language.description: BCP-47 language code of the document (e.g. en, de, fr, la).

image_url.description: URL to the scanned image of the original document.

source_url.description: Canonical URL of the source (e.g. https://archive.org/details/...).

digital_object_id.description: Persistent identifier such as ARK, DOI, or Internet Archive identifier.

repository.description: Name of the archive or repository holding the original document.

extraction_confidence.description: Confidence that the transcription accurately reflects the original document.

extracted_by.description: Agent ID or contributor ID that produced this RawRecord.

sensitive_data_redacted.description: True if SSNs, medical data, or other sensitive fields were removed.

redaction_notes.description: Description of what was redacted and why.
